message:
refactor(ondisk): BtrfsTreeWalker struct + ext4 dx_root reserved-field validation

Two related cleanups in the on-disk parser layer that sit on the read
side of the filesystem and did not need to share state beyond the
walk call.

crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs:

- Collapses the recursive walk_tree function's six trailing
  &mut Vec / &mut HashSet parameters into a BtrfsTreeWalker struct
  that owns the output buffer, active-path set, visited-nodes set,
  plus the borrowed read_physical callback and chunk array. The
  public walk_tree entry point builds the walker, calls
  walker.walk_node(root_logical), and returns walker.out, which
  keeps the existing signature stable for every caller. Internal
  recursion now calls self.walk_node(kp.blockptr) instead of
  threading the context tuple through a standalone helper, so
  every future addition to the walker (e.g. a depth cap or a
  per-edge callback) lives on one type instead of on a flat
  parameter list.
- Header parse-and-validate blocks are lifted into
  BtrfsHeader::parse_from_block() / header.validate(...) to kill
  the duplicated magic checks between leaf and internal cases.

crates/ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs:

- parse_dx_root now rejects non-zero bytes in the reserved_zero
  field (offset 0x18, u32) and the unused_flags byte (offset
  0x1F). Both fields are defined as "must be zero" by the Linux
  ext4 dx-hash indexed-directory layout, and silently accepting
  stray bits there would hide corrupted or forged HTree roots
  from our verifier. ParseError::InvalidField now names the
  rejected field in both cases so harness diagnostics stay
  specific.
- Factors the shared dx-root test fixture into
  make_dx_root_test_block() so parse_dx_root_basic and the new
  reserved-field rejection tests (parsed in follow-up test
  additions) share a single, auditable byte layout.
